title: Piston Push (Single)
description: A piston extends to push a block one space when powered and retracts when the signal is removed. Sticky pistons pull the block back on retraction. The piston's facing determines push direction — rotate it before powering. Cannot push obsidian, bedrock, or extended pistons.

steps.title: Stone base
steps.explanation: Three stone blocks in a line. The piston goes on the middle stone, the moveable block on the right.

steps.title: Lever and piston
steps.explanation: Lever on the left stone (the power source). Sticky piston on the middle stone facing east — it will push and pull the target block.

steps.title: Target block
steps.explanation: Place a coloured wool block directly in front of the piston head. Flip the lever — the piston pushes it one block east. Flip back — it pulls it back. This mechanism is the basis for piston doors, flying machines, and item movers.
